mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent recession, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing that drives negative S&P 500 returns within a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and payroll employment confirms robust consumer demand and business confidence, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing historical trends indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency, which supports equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3% forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when inflation crosses 3%, or the signal fails to capture volatility spikes during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and real GDP confirms broad-based economic growth, driving fundamental equity valuation upgrades and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll growth reinforces labor market tightness, supporting consumer spending expectations and driving short-term equity momentum over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns across all regimes, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1 million units signals a credit or demand shock, triggering defensive equity positioning and driving negative S&P 500 returns within a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX does not decline when HOUST drops below 1000, or declines are not statistically significant compared to baseline periods.
